stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant large language model.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its current popularity and performance tracking.
The associated tags confirm its relevance within the transformers and text-generation AI ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of NLP/Generative AI, specifically using the 'text-generation' pipeline.
The model is associated with key technologies like transformers and safetensors, indicating its technical nature.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: Introduces SchGen, the first large language model capable of generating editable PCB schematics from natural language.
Addresses the current bottleneck in electronics design, which is manual and expertise-intensive for PCB schematic creation.
Overcomes key challenges in the field by developing an LLM-suited representation and addressing data scarcity.

stories.title: GPIC: A Giant Permissive Image Corpus for Visual Generation
stories.author: Li Fei-Fei
bullet_points: Introduction of GPIC, a massive image corpus containing approximately 28 trillion pixels.
The corpus is designed for visual generative modeling, featuring 1M test examples and state-of-the-art captioning.
A key feature is that all images are permissively licensed for both research and commercial use.

stories.title: Opus 4.8 ships Dynamic Workflows — hundreds of parallel subagents per session. Read this before you wire it into prod.
stories.author: layzerzero105
bullet_points: Anthropic's Opus 4.8 introduces 'Dynamic Workflows,' enabling hundreds of parallel subagents per session.
The article provides a deep dive into the feature's actual capabilities, limitations, and potential cost pitfalls.
It offers critical advice on configuration settings to manage billing and optimize production use.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating a focus on large language model capabilities.
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern NLP/AI ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads for Mac users.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development and adoption.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a new personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core value proposition is running AI agents entirely on the user's own device for privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It supports running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no manual environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models come in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license.
The partnership involves ROOST (Robust Open Online Safety Tools) to enhance open-source safety capabilities.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon introduces Data Mixture Surgery (DMS) to analyze the hidden pretraining data composition of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The research addresses the lack of transparency regarding LLM 'digital DNA' (pretraining data mixture), which dictates model behavior and failures.
The tool estimates the domain-level distribution of an LLM's corpus using only the text generated by the target model.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of current LLM reasoning, where intermediate steps are tied to external token generation.
It proposes a method inspired by human cognition, allowing LLMs to use internal 'working memory' for manipulation.
This approach aims to decouple internal computation from the autoregressive generation process to improve reasoning fidelity.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: Addresses a critical failure mode in multi-component LLM agents where local coherence does not guarantee global correctness.
Introduces a formal measure, the 'compositional residual eps*', to quantify this 'locally coherent, globally incoherent' failure.
Provides a runtime computable metric based on L2 distance from the composed output to a joint coherent polytope.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: LLMSurgeon addresses the lack of transparency regarding the pretraining data mixture ('digital DNA') of Large Language Models (LLMs).
The research formalizes 'Data Mixture Surgery (DMS)' to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's corpus using only its generated text.
This work provides a novel method for post-hoc auditing and understanding the composition and potential biases within proprietary LLMs.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: SchGen is a novel LLM designed to generate editable Printed Circuit Board (PCB) schematics directly from natural language requests.
The research addresses a critical gap in hardware design, moving PCB schematic generation from manual, expertise-intensive processes toward AI automation.
The core technical challenge addressed is developing an LLM-suited representation and acquiring the necessary large-scale dataset for this domain.

stories.title: LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Introduces LLMSurgeon, a method to estimate the domain-level distribution of an LLM's pretraining corpus.
Addresses the critical, yet often hidden, nature of LLM 'digital DNA' (pretraining data mixture) which dictates model behavior.
Formalizes Data Mixture Surgery (DMS) to allow for post-hoc auditing and understanding of LLM data provenance using only generated text.

stories.title: SchGen: PCB Schematic Generation with Semantic-Grounded Code Representations
stories.author: Lili Qiu
bullet_points: Introduces SchGen, the first LLM designed to generate editable PCB schematics directly from natural language.
Addresses a significant industry bottleneck: PCB schematic design is currently manual and highly expertise-dependent.
Overcomes key challenges in the field, specifically the lack of LLM-suited representations and large-scale datasets for this task.

stories.title: Locally Coherent, Globally Incoherent: Bounding Compositional Incoherence in Multi-Component LLM Agents
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: Addresses a critical failure mode in multi-component LLM agents where local coherence does not guarantee global correctness.
Introduces a formal metric, the compositional residual (eps*), to quantify this 'locally coherent, globally incoherent' failure.
Provides a runtime computable method to bound this incoherence using L2 distance from a joint coherent polytope.

stories.title: MedCase-Structured: A Text-to-FHIR Dataset for Benchmarking Diagnostic Reasoning in Clinically Realistic EHR Settings
stories.author: Ziquan Fu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in LLM evaluation for healthcare by creating a text-to-FHIR dataset.
Focuses on benchmarking diagnostic reasoning in clinically realistic Electronic Health Record (EHR) settings.
Introduces a pipeline to generate structured HL7 FHIR R4 bundles from unstructured clinical text.

stories.title: Accenture to acquire Ookla
stories.author: Garbage
bullet_points: Accenture is reportedly acquiring Ookla, a company known for its speed test and monitoring services.
This acquisition signals a major strategic move by Accenture into the telecommunications and internet performance monitoring sector.
The deal involves the sale of Downdetector/Ookla to the IT service provider, indicating a shift in market consolidation.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, an open-source framework for creating personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, a popular tool for running local LLMs.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental local image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for macOS users.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code functionality within Ollama.
It facilitates the use of advanced models like Claude alongside open-source models.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code locally.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives like gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Microsoft is threatening legal action for disclosing exploits
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Microsoft is under scrutiny regarding its handling of zero-day exploits.
A figure named 'Nightmare Eclipse' is publicly demonstrating exploit code against Microsoft.
The focus of the reporting is on Microsoft's response to these public disclosures, as noted by cybersecurity researcher Kevin Beaumont.

stories.title: Botnet of more than 17 million devices dismantled
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A large-scale botnet, comprising over 17 million devices, has been successfully dismantled.
The infrastructure supporting the botnet was reportedly linked to a Russia-based residential proxy network.
The story falls under cybersecurity, indicating a significant, though not necessarily breakthrough, security event.

stories.title: Physics Is All You Need? A Case Study in Physicist-Supervised AI Development of Scientific Software
stories.author: Nhat-Minh Nguyen
bullet_points: The study investigates the role of AI agents (Claude models) in scientific software development under physicist supervision.
A case study tracked 12 work days and 57 sessions to build a differentiable perturbation theory module (CLAX-PT) in JAX.
The analysis quantified supervision events, showing the AI autonomously resolved ten issues, supplemented by physicist domain knowledge.

stories.title: VideoMLA: Low-Rank Latent KV Cache for Minute-Scale Autoregressive Video Diffusion
stories.author: Pinar Yanardag
bullet_points: Introduces VideoMLA, a new approach for video diffusion models.
Focuses on optimizing the memory and latency associated with the Key-Value (KV) cache in autoregressive video generation.
Replaces per-head keys and values with a shared low-rank latent attention mechanism (MLA).

stories.title: Tiny but Trusted: Efficient Vision-Language Reasoning for Time-Series Anomaly Detection
stories.author: Ismini Lourentzou
bullet_points: Addresses the limitation of current Vision-Language Models (VLMs) in detecting anomalies within sequential (time-series) data.
Highlights a key gap in anomaly detection benchmarks: the lack of natural-language rationales for detected anomalies.
Introduces VisAnomBench, a new benchmark designed to enable VLMs to produce grounded and interpretable anomaly detection decisions.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: A new approach suggests that reasoning capabilities in large language models can be achieved by sampling from a 'power distribution' of the base model.
This method potentially bypasses the need for extensive post-training, reinforcement learning, curated datasets, or external verifiers.
The primary technical hurdle for practical application is developing an efficient sampler capable of mixing into this power distribution.

stories.title: DynaFLIP: Rethinking Robotics Perception via Tri-Modal-Dynamics Guided Representation
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: DynaFLIP is a new dynamics-aware multimodal pre-training framework designed to improve robot perception.
It addresses a limitation in current robotics pipelines where motion understanding is often left to downstream policies after static visual encoding.
The method integrates image, language, and 3D flow triplets to push motion understanding upstream into the perception stage.

stories.title: Efficient Test-Time Finetuning of LLMs via Convex Reconstruction and Gradient Caching
stories.author: Alaa Maalouf
bullet_points: The paper addresses the computational bottleneck of Test-Time Finetuning (TTFT) in Large Language Models (LLMs), which is currently slow due to per-query selection and finetuning.
It introduces 'HullFT,' a new method designed to make TTFT practical by improving efficiency.
The research focuses on balancing the trade-off between speed and quality in prompt adaptation for LLMs.

stories.title: Fairness-Aware Federated Learning with Trajectory Shapley Value
stories.author: Ziqi Wang
bullet_points: Addresses limitations in Federated Learning (FL) regarding biased and unstable learning due to fixed aggregation weights.
Proposes the Trajectory Shapley Value (TSV) to improve fairness and stability in FL settings.
Focuses on improving collaborative model training across heterogeneous and privacy-sensitive client data.

stories.title: SoundnessBench: Can Your AI Scientist Really Tell Good Research Ideas from Bad Ones?
stories.author: Furong Huang
bullet_points: Introduces SoundnessBench, a new benchmark designed to test an AI's ability to judge the methodological viability of ML research ideas.
Addresses a critical bottleneck in autonomous AI research agents: distinguishing good research proposals from poor ones before resource expenditure.
The benchmark is constructed from 1,099 machine learning research proposals originally submitted to ICLR.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: The paper proposes an alternative to standard reinforcement learning fine-tuning for improving reasoning in large language models.
It introduces the concept of a 'power distribution' derived from the base model's distribution, which can elicit strong reasoning capabilities.
The main practical challenge identified is the need for an efficient sampling method to effectively 'mix' into this power distribution.

stories.title: In-Context Reward Adaptation for Robust Preference Modeling
stories.author: Ermin Wei
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) where static reward models fail to capture diverse human preferences.
The proposed method introduces 'In-Context Reward Adaptation' to allow models to generalize to unseen preference domains.
This work aims to overcome the limitations of existing multi-reward frameworks that are restricted to fixed, known domains.

stories.title: Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of current LLM reasoning, which relies on externalizing intermediate thoughts via token generation.
It proposes a method inspired by human cognition, suggesting internal working memory for information manipulation without external communication.
The goal is to decouple internal computation from the autoregressive generation process to enhance LLM reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: COMPOSE: Composing Future Theorems from Citations and Formal Structure
stories.author: Michael Werman
bullet_points: Introduces 'COMPOSE,' a new method for generating plausible future mathematical theorems.
The approach addresses the limitations of prior work by simultaneously considering both the direction of prior research and formal structural dependencies.
This work aims to create 'grounded future mathematical generation' for anchor papers using two complementary sources.

stories.title: Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points
stories.author: Quanquan C. Liu
bullet_points: The paper proposes an alternative to standard Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning for advanced reasoning models.
It suggests using a 'power distribution' derived from the base model's sampling to achieve comparable reasoning without extra training or datasets.
The main technical challenge identified is the need for an efficient sampler to accurately draw from this novel power distribution.

stories.title: Resolution Diagnostics for Paired LLM Evaluation
stories.author: Anany Kotawala
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical issue in LLM evaluation: many existing pairwise rankings on public leaderboards are statistically unresolved.
Specific examples show that 11 of 40 comparisons on the Open LLM Leaderboard v1 and 4 of 9 MMLU-Pro pairs fail to meet standard resolution targets.
The research proposes a framework to better frame and resolve paired LLM evaluations, suggesting methodological improvements for benchmarking.

stories.title: Microsoft degrades functionality of perpetually-licensed offline products
stories.author: antipurist
bullet_points: Microsoft is reportedly degrading functionality of its perpetually-licensed, offline products.
This suggests a potential shift in Microsoft's software licensing or support strategy.
The news originates from Hacker News, indicating interest within the developer/tech community.

stories.title: I Made My AI Models Argue, Then Let Hermes Be the Judge
stories.author: arqamwd
bullet_points: The article describes a novel $0 multi-model decision agent.
It utilizes three Large Language Models (LLMs) to engage in a debate.
A model named Hermes is employed to act as the judge to determine trustworthiness.

stories.title: Octorato: an open-source AI agent OS with built-in per-client FinOps
stories.author: carloscape
bullet_points: Octorato is an open-source AI agent operating system (OS).
It addresses a limitation in existing agent frameworks by supporting multi-client deployments.
A key feature is built-in per-client FinOps (Financial Operations).
